name: Glasmästarsill (Glassblower's Herring)
recipeIngredient: Salted herring; 1 to 1.5 lbs each, cleaned and scraped
Fresh horse radish root; scraped and thinly sliced
Prepared horseradish; drained and squeezed dry in a kitchen towel
Onions; preferably red, peeled and thinly sliced (3/4 cup)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine vinegar, water, and sugar in a 1 to 1.5 quart enameled or stainless-steel saucepan. Bring to a boil and stir constantly until the sugar completely dissolves.
2. Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.
3. Wash herring in cold running water and cut into 1-inch thick pieces.
4. Arrange a layer of onions at the bottom of a 1-quart glass jar (Mason jar, if possible) with a tightly fitting lid.
5. Add a few slices of herring, carrot, ginger root, horseradish, allspice, mustard seeds, and bay leaves on top.
6. Repeat layers until all ingredients are used, making about 3-4 layers.
7. Pour the cool pickling liquid into the jar, ensuring it covers the contents.
8. Securely close the jar and refrigerate for 2 to 3 days before serving as an appetizer or part of a smörgåsbord.
